mainEntity.name: How much does an AJ20P6 (Ingenium 3.0 I6 Petrol / P400) engine replacement cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned AJ20P6 Ingenium petrol typically costs £4,500–£7,500 supply only, varying by specification and rebuild quality. Used units can be found from £2,500–£4,500, but provenance is critical. Fitting adds £1,000–£2,000 depending on labour rates.

mainEntity.name: Is the AJ20P6 (Ingenium 3.0 I6 Petrol / P400) a reliable engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: The AJ20P6 is a modern and capable straight-six petrol (4.0★) with positive real-world feedback. Early examples have reported cooling system issues, but JLR has addressed many of these with software updates and revisions. Regular maintenance is essential.

mainEntity.name: What is the most common AJ20P6 failure?

acceptedAnswer.text: Cooling system issues are the most common reported problems — including water pump leaks, coolant hose failures, and occasional thermostat housing cracks. Other reported issues include oil leaks from the timing cover and occasional software glitches in the MHEV system.

mainEntity.name: Which Land Rover models use the AJ20P6?

acceptedAnswer.text: The AJ20P6 (P400) powers the latest-generation Range Rover (L460), Range Rover Sport (L461), and Defender (L663).

mainEntity.name: Is the Land Rover AJ20P6 the same as Jaguar's version?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — the AJ20P6 is shared with Jaguar fitments in the F-Pace, XF, and XJ. The engine is mechanically identical, though ECU calibrations and ancillaries differ by model.
